tuition_fee.note: B.Arch tuition fees for Government, Government Cost-Sharing, and Self-financing colleges are NOT specified in the 2026 prospectus. CEE will publish the fee structure separately before CAP-2026 begins. (Clause 12.1)

concessions.name: SC / ST fee exemption
concessions.description: Candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes are fully exempted from payment of fees, whether allotted on merit or on reserved seats.

concessions.name: OEC fee exemption
concessions.description: Other Eligible Communities (OEC) candidates listed in Annexure X(b) are exempted from fee payment under the same conditions as SC/ST.

concessions.name: SEBC fee exemption (income up to Rs. 6 lakhs)
concessions.description: SEBC candidates listed in Annexure X(a) whose annual family income is up to Rs. 6 lakhs are exempted from payment of fees at the time of allotment.
eligibility: Income certificate showing annual family income <= Rs. 6 lakhs

concessions.name: Tuition Fee Waiver Scheme (income up to Rs. 8 lakhs)
concessions.description: Children of parents whose annual income from all sources is up to Rs. 8 lakhs are eligible for the Tuition Fee Waiver. Only the tuition fee component is waived; all other fees are payable.

concessions.name: Fishermen (FM) children fee exemption
concessions.description: Children of registered fishermen, on production of the prescribed certificate, are exempted from payment of fees.

concessions.name: Inmates of Sri Chitra / Nirbhaya / Juvenile Homes
concessions.description: Candidates who are inmates of Sri Chitra Home, Nirbhaya Home, or notified Juvenile Homes are fully exempted from payment of fees on production of the certificate from the institution.

refund_rules.title: Withdrawal before classes start
refund_rules.description: If a student withdraws before the start of classes, the entire fee already paid is refunded after deduction of a processing fee of not more than Rs. 1,000.

refund_rules.title: Withdrawal after joining (vacated seat is filled)
refund_rules.description: If a student leaves after joining the course and the vacated seat is filled within the last date of admission, the institution refunds the fee after deducting Rs. 1,000 plus proportionate monthly fee and hostel rent.

refund_rules.title: No fee for subsequent years on cancellation
refund_rules.description: Institutions cannot demand fees for subsequent years from a candidate who cancels admission. The refund of fees and certificates must be completed within 7 days.

refund_rules.title: Liquidated damages for unfilled options after final phase
refund_rules.description: Candidates who file or retain unnecessary options but do not take admission after a final-phase allotment lose any fee already paid to CEE if the resulting vacancy stays unfilled.
